top_stories.title: eGovPH back online; no security breach, says DICT
top_stories.summary: MANILA, Philippines — Access to the government services mobile app eGovPH has been restored on Friday following a two-day downtime due to a surge in user activity, the Department of Information and Communications Technology (DICT) said. eGovPH underwent maintenance and feature updates on April 13, and has since expe...

top_stories.title: Meralco’s P4-B cost recovery cleared by regulator
top_stories.summary: MANILA, Philippines — Millions of consumers of Manila Electric Co. (Meralco) will have to brace for higher power rates beginning September after regulators gave the green light for the recovery of more than P4 billion linked to a subsidiary’s gas plant. top_stories.title: ICTSI braces for fuel supply uncertainty
top_stories.summary: MANILA, Philippines — International Container Terminal Services Inc. (ICTSI) said fuel supply remains stable for now, but warned that the outlook is increasingly uncertain as the war in the Middle East approached its eighth week. top_stories.title: IMF says Philippines faces ‘difficult situation’ as Mideast energy shocks weigh on growth
top_stories.summary: WASHINGTON, D.C. — The Philippines is facing a difficult situation as its heavy reliance on oil imports tests its economic resilience amid the ongoing energy crisis from the Middle East war, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) said.

secondary_signals.title: Converge fast-tracks shift to electric vehicle fleet
secondary_signals.summary: Broadband provider Converge ICT Solutions Inc. is expediting its transition to an electric vehicle fleet to shield its installation, maintenance and repair efforts from oil price shocks.

secondary_signals.title: Diesel, gasoline prices seen sliding some more
secondary_signals.summary: MANILA, Philippines — Another round of price rollbacks for diesel and gasoline is expected next week, by as much as P19 per liter, amid hopes that the war in the Middle East is easing. secondary_signals.title: Luzon, Visayas electricity grids placed under ‘yellow’ alert
secondary_signals.summary: Updated on April 16, 2026 at 6:59 p.m. MANILA, Philippines — The National Grid Corp. of the Philippines (NGCP) has placed the Luzon and Visayas grids under yellow alert on Thursday, its second issuance for the year, as some power plants tripped.
